exam_price_notes: INR 200 (~$2.40 USD). Exemption for SC/ST and female candidates. OBC: No exemption but reservation in merit applies.

testing_centers.notes: Offline paper-based (OMR) examination conducted at designated centres in major cities across all states and union territories of India. Centre allocation done by UPSC; candidates cannot choose specific centres.

id_requirements: Valid government-issued photo ID required on exam day: Aadhaar card, passport, voter ID, or driving licence. Admit card (hall ticket) is mandatory and must be downloaded from the UPSC OTR portal before the exam.
exam_rules: OMR-based objective papers (where applicable). Negative marking: 1/3 mark deducted per wrong answer. Unattempted questions carry no penalty. No calculators or electronic devices permitted. Dark blue or black ballpoint pen only (not gel or ink pens). Candidates must report 30 minutes before commencement. Mobile phones strictly prohibited.

prerequisites.description: Indian citizen. Age: 21-30 years (relaxation for reserved categories). Bachelor's degree in Engineering (B.E./B.Tech) or equivalent in relevant stream. Final year engineering students also eligible to apply.

official_study_resources.title: UPSC Official Website
official_study_resources.notes: Syllabus, previous year papers, notifications, and e-admit cards.

official_study_resources.title: UPSC Previous Year Question Papers
official_study_resources.notes: Free official repository of all previous year question papers.
